## Results

The aligned per-protocol design has no clean randomised treatment coefficient.
Every coefficient below, including the cohort contrast, is an adjusted association.

This section reports how each predictor tracks the aligned post-score. Because the cohort was defined by who actually followed the protocol rather than by random assignment, no term here is causal; each one is an association that could be driven by other differences between children.

```{python}
# | echo: false
factor_summary = _csv("factor_summary.csv")
factor_summary if factor_summary is not None else "No factor summary."
```

_How to read the factor summary:_ each row is one predictor. `median` is the posterior median of its coefficient (the typical association after adjustment), `lo`/`hi` are the ends of the credible interval (the plausible range), and `prob_positive` is the share of posterior mass above zero (near 1 means the association is reliably positive, near 0 reliably negative, near 0.5 undecided).

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
if factor_summary is not None and "role" in factor_summary.columns:
    _assoc = factor_summary[factor_summary.role != "causal"]
    if len(_assoc):
        print('::: {.callout-important title="All terms are associations"}\n')
        print(
            "The per-protocol cohort term is confounded by age-at-onset and timing. "
            "Read coefficients as associated with the aligned post-score, never as "
            "randomised treatment effects.\n"
        )
        _cols = [c for c in ["term", "median", "lo", "hi", "prob_positive"] if c in _assoc.columns]
        print(_assoc[_cols].to_markdown(index=False))
        print("\n:::")
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print(_img("association_forest.png", "Adjusted-association coefficients"))
print(
    "\n_How to read the forest plot:_ the marker is the posterior median and the "
    "lines its credible intervals (thick = inner 50%, thin = 89%). The dashed "
    "line at 0 is no-effect; posterior mass to its right favours benefit, to its "
    "left harm; a wider line means more uncertainty. Every term here is an "
    "adjusted association, not a randomised effect."
)
```

### Cohort Marginal

This is the aligned immediate-vs-wait-list gap on the items scale, summarising the cohort contrast in words a reader can act on.

```{python}
# | echo: false
cohort_marginal = _csv("cohort_marginal.csv")
cohort_marginal if cohort_marginal is not None else "No cohort marginal table."
```

_How to read the cohort marginal:_ `trt_items_median` is the typical difference in items between the immediate and wait-list cohorts (positive favours immediate), `trt_items_lo`/`trt_items_hi` bound the plausible range, and `prob_trt_pos` is the probability that the gap is above zero. It is per-protocol, so read it as association, not a randomised effect.

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
if cohort_marginal is not None:
    _c = cohort_marginal.iloc[0]
    print(
        f"Aligned immediate-vs-wait-list marginal: median "
        f"{_c.trt_items_median:+.1f} items "
        f"(50% CrI {_c.trt_items_lo50:+.1f} to {_c.trt_items_hi50:+.1f}; "
        f"89% {_c.trt_items_lo:+.1f} to {_c.trt_items_hi:+.1f}); "
        f"P(> 0) = {_c.prob_trt_pos:.3f}. This is per-protocol and not randomised."
    )
```
